info.title: Subscribe API
info.description: APIs to manage the subscription service

post.description:
This resource will create an event subscription for a user. It returns a list of event subscriptions. To create a subscription for an event, your client must have access to the event's associated api. The response will include links to:-
  • user: The subscribed user provided by the current authorization context.
  • self: The created subscription.

parameters.name: eventTypeId
parameters.description: See Event Types for valid event type names.

parameters.name: filters1
parameters.description: List of MetadataFilters to filter events on metadata.

parameters.name: targetEndpoint
parameters.description: The postback endpoint that receives the event(s).

parameters.name: status4
parameters.description: The status of the event subscription. Only a status of Active can be specified on subscription creation.

parameters.name: displayName
parameters.description: Human-readable name to easily identify the event subscription.

parameters.name: token
parameters.description: A string that was sent with each delivery to validate the sender.

user.description: The link back to the subscribed user.

self.description: The link to the created subscription.

id.description: Event Subscription ID as a GUID.

eventTypeId.description: See Event Types for valid event names

contentType.description: The request body used to create or update a client

get.description: This resource will return a paged list of event subscriptions for the user. The endpoint will return all Active, Expired, and Terminated subscriptions.

contentType.description: The request body used to create or update a client

get.description:
This resource will get a single event subscription by id. The response will include links to:
  • user: The subscribed user provided by the current authorization context.
  • self: The subscription itself.

parameters.name: id
parameters.description: Event Subscription ID as a GUID.

user.description: The link back to the subscribed user.

self.description: The link to the created subscription.

HTTPTargetEndpointAuthorizationHeader.name: Authorization
HTTPTargetEndpointAuthorizationHeader.description: If set via the eventSubscriptionDelivery endpoint, we will include an Authorization header on every HTTP Post callback for this client with the complete content provided in this property. For more information, see RFC 7235, section 4.2 and RCF 7617. You may choose to rotate this value on a regular basis. The max size for this value is 4 kb.

schema.description: If provided, we will include an authorization header on every HTTP Post callback for this client with the complete content provided in this property.
